untranslatable_reason: The signal requires calculation of a custom spread between two assets, Z-score computation relative to a moving average and standard deviation of that spread, and position sizing proportional to the negative Z-score. These require multi-asset correlation, custom spread calculations, and dynamic position sizing based on volatility-adjusted Z-scores. The allowed indicator set does not support: (1) inter-asset spreads with hedge ratios, (2) Z-score calculations on custom spreads, (3) moving standard deviation of spreads, or (4) position sizing proportional to Z-scores. This is a pairs/statistical arbitrage strategy requiring specialized indicators not available in the standard indicator list.
translation_notes: Signal is a sophisticated pairs trading strategy (Chande mean reversion on spreads). Attempted to approximate using returns as a proxy for spread mean reversion, but this fundamentally misrepresents the strategy. A proper implementation requires: custom spread calculation between two correlated assets, rolling mean and standard deviation of that spread, Z-score normalization, and dynamic position sizing. The backtest engine would need to support multi-asset rules and custom ratio calculations.
